feed.title: Ed's
feed.subtitle: Hi there! Welcome to my personal website. I'm an AI researcher with experience in both academia and startups. I co-founded Future Frame in San Francisco and Paris, where we used advanced transformer models to transform data science by removing biases from traditional methods. Our API platform allowed data scientists to easily work with our innovations. I completed my PhD in Machine Learning at CentraleSupélec, focusing on making AI systems more secure and trustworthy, particularly in fields like computer vision and medical imaging. My background includes a research internship in Montreal and degrees from ISAE-SUPAERO and ITA in Brazil. I'm passionate about creating AI that's not only powerful but also truly human-centered and reliable. Thanks for stopping by to learn more about my work!.

entry.title: About
entry.content:
I am a PhD Candidate in Machine Learning at CentraleSupélec-Université Paris-Saclay working towards safe and robust AI under the supervision of Prof. Pablo Piantanida. My PhD is supported by the BPI France through the IBM Research project AIDA: Artificial Intelligence for Digital Automation. Through my work, I hope to contribute to the betterment of society by developing responsible and safe algorithms.

In 2023, I was selected to participate in the Nordic Probabilistic AI School (ProbAI) in Trondheim, Norway. In 2022, I was awarded the MITACS Globalink scholarship for a four-month research internship at MILA and ÉTS in Montreal, QC, Canada. In 2021, I got my first first-author paper accepted at (ICLR2022).

Previously, I obtained my bachelor's degree from the Instituto Tecnológico de Aeronáutica (ITA) in Brazil. ITA is the top-1 engineering school in Brazil with a selective admission process with an acceptance rate of 2%.

In 2018, I was awarded an excellence scholarship to pursue my master's degree at ISAE-SUPAERO in Toulouse, France. There, I was selected for the Data Science and Applied Mathematics major. During my master's, I was involved in a research project at the intersection of machine learning and communication systems leading to my first journal publication.

As a fun side project, I co-developed promptriddle, a challenging enigma solving game on an emulated terminal with over 2000 players.
